testcase.name: test_get_env_vars_auth_with_github_app_installation_missing_inputs

failure.message: AssertionError: 'GH_TOKEN environment variable not set' != 'GH_APP_ID set and GH_APP_INSTALLATION_ID or [31 chars] set' - GH_TOKEN environment variable not set + GH_APP_ID set and GH_APP_INSTALLATION_ID or GH_APP_PRIVATE_KEY variable not set

testcase.name: test_get_env_vars_missing_organization

failure.message: AssertionError: 'GH_TOKEN environment variable not set' != 'ORGANIZATION environment variable not set' - GH_TOKEN environment variable not set ? ^^ -- + ORGANIZATION environment variable not set ? ++ ^^^^^ +

testcase.name: test_get_env_vars_no_organization_set

failure.message: AssertionError: 'GH_TOKEN environment variable not set' != 'ORGANIZATION environment variable not set' - GH_TOKEN environment variable not set ? ^^ -- + ORGANIZATION environment variable not set ? ++ ^^^^^ +

testcase.name: test_get_env_vars_optional_values

failure.message: ValueError: GH_TOKEN environment variable not set

testcase.failure:
self = <test_env.TestEnv testMethod=test_get_env_vars_optional_values>
@patch.dict( os.environ, { "GH_ACTOR": "testactor", "GH_APP_ID": "", "GH_APP_INSTALLATION_ID": "", "GH_APP_PRIVATE_KEY": "", "GITHUB_APP_ENTERPRISE_ONLY": "", "GH_ENTERPRISE_URL": "testghe", "GH_TOKEN": TOKEN, "ORGANIZATION": ORGANIZATION, "PR_BODY": BODY, "PR_TITLE": TITLE, "REPOS_JSON_LOCATION": "test/repos.json", }, ) def test_get_env_vars_optional_values(self): """Test that optional values are set to their default values if not provided""" expected_result = EnvVars( "testactor", None, None, b"", False, "testghe", TOKEN, ORGANIZATION, BODY, TITLE, "test/repos.json", ) > result = get_env_vars(True) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
test_env.py:137: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
test = True
def get_env_vars(test: bool = False) -> EnvVars: """ Get the environment variables for use in the action.
Args: test (bool): Whether or not to load the environment variables from a .env file (default: False)
Returns: gh_actor (str): The GitHub actor to use for authentication gh_app_id (int | None): The GitHub App ID to use for authentication gh_app_installation_id (int | None): The GitHub App Installation ID to use for authentication gh_app_private_key_bytes (bytes): The GitHub App Private Key as bytes to use for authentication gh_app_enterprise_only (bool): Set this to true if the GH APP is created on GHE and needs to communicate with GHE api only gh_enterprise_url (str): The GitHub Enterprise URL to use for authentication gh_token (str | None): The GitHub token to use for authentication organization (str): The GitHub organization to use for the PR pr_body (str): The PR body to use for the PR pr_title (str): The PR title to use for the PR repos_json_location (str): The location of the repos.json file """ gh_actor = settings.get("GH_ACTOR", "nobody") gh_app_id = get_int_env_var("GH_APP_ID") gh_app_private_key_bytes = settings.get("GH_APP_PRIVATE_KEY", "").encode("utf8") gh_app_installation_id = get_int_env_var("GH_APP_INSTALLATION_ID") gh_app_enterprise_only = get_bool_env_var("GITHUB_APP_ENTERPRISE_ONLY")
if gh_app_id and (not gh_app_private_key_bytes or not gh_app_installation_id): raise ValueError( "GH_APP_ID set and GH_APP_INSTALLATION_ID or GH_APP_PRIVATE_KEY variable not set" )
gh_token = settings.get("GH_TOKEN") if ( not gh_app_id and not gh_app_private_key_bytes and not gh_app_installation_id and not gh_token ): > raise ValueError("GH_TOKEN environment variable not set") E ValueError: GH_TOKEN environment variable not set
env.py:149: ValueError

testcase.name: test_get_env_vars_pr_body_too_long

failure.message: AssertionError: 'GH_TOKEN environment variable not set' != 'BODY environment variable is too long. Max 65536 characters' - GH_TOKEN environment variable not set + BODY environment variable is too long. Max 65536 characters

testcase.name: test_get_env_vars_pr_title_too_long

failure.message: AssertionError: 'GH_TOKEN environment variable not set' != 'PR_TITLE environment variable is too long. Max 70 characters' - GH_TOKEN environment variable not set + PR_TITLE environment variable is too long. Max 70 characters

testcase.name: test_get_env_vars_with_github_app

failure.message: ValueError: GH_TOKEN environment variable not set

testcase.failure:
self = <test_env.TestEnv testMethod=test_get_env_vars_with_github_app>
@patch.dict( os.environ, { "GH_ACTOR": "", "GH_APP_ID": "12345", "GH_APP_INSTALLATION_ID": "678910", "GH_APP_PRIVATE_KEY": "hello", "GITHUB_APP_ENTERPRISE_ONLY": "", "GH_ENTERPRISE_URL": "", "GH_TOKEN": "", "ORGANIZATION": ORGANIZATION, "PR_BODY": BODY, "PR_TITLE": TITLE, }, clear=True, ) def test_get_env_vars_with_github_app(self): """Test that all environment variables are set correctly using github app authentication""" expected_result = EnvVars( "", 12345, 678910, b"hello", False, "", "", ORGANIZATION, BODY, TITLE, REPOS_JSON_LOCATION, ) > result = get_env_vars(True) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
test_env.py:103: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
test = True
def get_env_vars(test: bool = False) -> EnvVars: """ Get the environment variables for use in the action.
Args: test (bool): Whether or not to load the environment variables from a .env file (default: False)
Returns: gh_actor (str): The GitHub actor to use for authentication gh_app_id (int | None): The GitHub App ID to use for authentication gh_app_installation_id (int | None): The GitHub App Installation ID to use for authentication gh_app_private_key_bytes (bytes): The GitHub App Private Key as bytes to use for authentication gh_app_enterprise_only (bool): Set this to true if the GH APP is created on GHE and needs to communicate with GHE api only gh_enterprise_url (str): The GitHub Enterprise URL to use for authentication gh_token (str | None): The GitHub token to use for authentication organization (str): The GitHub organization to use for the PR pr_body (str): The PR body to use for the PR pr_title (str): The PR title to use for the PR repos_json_location (str): The location of the repos.json file """ gh_actor = settings.get("GH_ACTOR", "nobody") gh_app_id = get_int_env_var("GH_APP_ID") gh_app_private_key_bytes = settings.get("GH_APP_PRIVATE_KEY", "").encode("utf8") gh_app_installation_id = get_int_env_var("GH_APP_INSTALLATION_ID") gh_app_enterprise_only = get_bool_env_var("GITHUB_APP_ENTERPRISE_ONLY")
if gh_app_id and (not gh_app_private_key_bytes or not gh_app_installation_id): raise ValueError( "GH_APP_ID set and GH_APP_INSTALLATION_ID or GH_APP_PRIVATE_KEY variable not set" )
gh_token = settings.get("GH_TOKEN") if ( not gh_app_id and not gh_app_private_key_bytes and not gh_app_installation_id and not gh_token ): > raise ValueError("GH_TOKEN environment variable not set") E ValueError: GH_TOKEN environment variable not set
env.py:149: ValueError

testcase.name: test_get_env_vars_with_token

failure.message: ValueError: GH_TOKEN environment variable not set

testcase.failure:
self = <test_env.TestEnv testMethod=test_get_env_vars_with_token>
@patch.dict( os.environ, { "GH_ACTOR": "", "GH_APP_ID": "", "GH_APP_INSTALLATION_ID": "", "GH_APP_PRIVATE_KEY": "", "GITHUB_APP_ENTERPRISE_ONLY": "", "GH_ENTERPRISE_URL": "", "GH_TOKEN": TOKEN, "ORGANIZATION": ORGANIZATION, "PR_BODY": BODY, "PR_TITLE": TITLE, }, ) def test_get_env_vars_with_token(self): """Test that all environment variables are set correctly using a token""" expected_result = EnvVars( "", None, None, b"", False, "", TOKEN, ORGANIZATION, BODY, TITLE, REPOS_JSON_LOCATION, ) > result = get_env_vars(True) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
test_env.py:69: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
test = True
def get_env_vars(test: bool = False) -> EnvVars: """ Get the environment variables for use in the action.
Args: test (bool): Whether or not to load the environment variables from a .env file (default: False)
Returns: gh_actor (str): The GitHub actor to use for authentication gh_app_id (int | None): The GitHub App ID to use for authentication gh_app_installation_id (int | None): The GitHub App Installation ID to use for authentication gh_app_private_key_bytes (bytes): The GitHub App Private Key as bytes to use for authentication gh_app_enterprise_only (bool): Set this to true if the GH APP is created on GHE and needs to communicate with GHE api only gh_enterprise_url (str): The GitHub Enterprise URL to use for authentication gh_token (str | None): The GitHub token to use for authentication organization (str): The GitHub organization to use for the PR pr_body (str): The PR body to use for the PR pr_title (str): The PR title to use for the PR repos_json_location (str): The location of the repos.json file """ gh_actor = settings.get("GH_ACTOR", "nobody") gh_app_id = get_int_env_var("GH_APP_ID") gh_app_private_key_bytes = settings.get("GH_APP_PRIVATE_KEY", "").encode("utf8") gh_app_installation_id = get_int_env_var("GH_APP_INSTALLATION_ID") gh_app_enterprise_only = get_bool_env_var("GITHUB_APP_ENTERPRISE_ONLY")
if gh_app_id and (not gh_app_private_key_bytes or not gh_app_installation_id): raise ValueError( "GH_APP_ID set and GH_APP_INSTALLATION_ID or GH_APP_PRIVATE_KEY variable not set" )
gh_token = settings.get("GH_TOKEN") if ( not gh_app_id and not gh_app_private_key_bytes and not gh_app_installation_id and not gh_token ): > raise ValueError("GH_TOKEN environment variable not set") E ValueError: GH_TOKEN environment variable not set
env.py:149: ValueError
